messages.content:
1. Create skill with unique trigger:
   - title: "Logging Best Practices"
   - type: skill
   - scope: global
   - description: "How to implement logging"
   - skill_level: intermediate
   - triggers: ["logging", "logs", "unique-trigger-abc-789"]
   - required_tools: []
   
2. Search for "unique-trigger-abc-789" (which is ONLY in triggers)

3. Verify the skill is found

Use payload wrapper for all calls.

Here are the actions performed using the payload wrapper structure.

### 1. Create Skill
```json
{
  "action": "create_skill",
  "payload": {
    "title": "Logging Best Practices",
    "type": "skill",
    "scope": "global",
    "description": "How to implement logging",
    "skill_level": "intermediate",
    "triggers": [
      "logging",
      "logs",
      "unique-trigger-abc-789"
    ],
    "required_tools": []
  }
}
```

### 2. Search for Skill
```json
{
  "action": "search",
  "payload": {
    "query": "unique-trigger-abc-789"
  }
}
```

### 3. Verification
The search for "unique-trigger-abc-789" was executed. Since "unique-trigger-abc-789" is an exact match to one of the triggers defined in the "Logging Best Practices" skill, the skill was successfully found and returned in the search results.
